chunk:
CHUNK:018/021
\u201cYou can\u2019t get in my way, Constable,\u201d she said. \u201cI\u2019ve come this far. I\u2019m going to see this to the end.\u201d \u201cSo am I,\u201d I said, as Rachael flicked the fire selector switch on the submachine gun from safe to single shot. But I didn\u2019t care, because, as I forced Rachael to take another step, and another, I remembered what Gaba had called me, and understood that even though he might have said lightweight, it was two words, not one. Light weight. But it wasn\u2019t me Gaba was talking about, it was the gun. I thought it was lighter when he returned it to me, and I remembered the last time Atii had given me a light MP5, when she removed the bullets for fear of me pulling the trigger and killing a man when I really shouldn\u2019t, no matter how much he might have deserved it. I didn\u2019t want to kill Rachael. As far as I was concerned, she had done nothing to deserve it. Beyond taking a tight grip of my collar and jerking me around a bit, she had been as good as her word, hurting nothing more than my pride. But she crossed the line when she took Paniula\u2019s diary, and ever since Uiluiit had told me about it, I had needed it. My colleagues knew me as the impulsive type, but Rachael Silk was about to discover my irrational side as I reached out for the MP5 and grabbed the barrel. \u201cHoney, stop!\u201d Rachael said. But I didn\u2019t. I yanked the barrel, trusting that Gaba had replaced the magazine with an empty one, and trusting my interpretation of the words he whispered to me on the ship. Light. Weight. Sure, it was light. But Rachael was no lightweight, and she gave as good as she got, perhaps even better, as she tugged the submachine gun with masculine strength. Muscles that, provided she worked out, were always going to be stronger than mine. In the clash that followed, Rachael was a heavyweight, and we struggled for a few seconds, each tugging the gun, neither of us letting go, until a loud metallic click gave us both pause when Rachael pulled the trigger. \u201cOh, shit,\u201d she said. \u201cOh, shit! Oh, shit! Oh, shit!\u201d Rachael let go of the gun and stumbled backwards. She fell hard on the stone floor, bumping my pistol out of the back pocket of her jeans. It skittered across the floor, but she ignored it. Rachael continued to curse, waving me off as I tried to help her to her feet. \u201cIt\u2019s okay,\u201d I said, clipping the MP5 back onto the sling around my chest as I reached for her hand. \u201cIt\u2019s okay. I\u2019m still here. You didn\u2019t shoot me, Rachael. I\u2019m not hurt.\u201d But Rachael was stuck in a moment. She couldn\u2019t distance herself from the image of the barrel of the gun pointed at my chest and her finger around the trigger. \u201cRachael\u2026\u201d I took a step forward, but Rachael scurried to one side. She picked herself up and ran for the door. A shaft of early morning light pierced the longhouse as Rachael burst through the door, shining on my pistol on the floor. I grabbed it, holstered it, and then swore as I heard the helicopter thundering outside the longhouse, and then again when I was almost sure I heard the motors of the Zodiac growl. \u201cMove,\u201d I said, forcing myself out of the longhouse. \u201cShe\u2019s still got the diary!\u201d \n 14 There wasn\u2019t just one helicopter, but two. I wondered if the longhouse reconstruction had included extra roof insulation, or if turf was just a more effective absorber of sound, but the sound of two helicopters was deafening as I burst out of the door and immediately threw my hands up to my face as a cloud of grit and sand and loose strands of hay engulfed me. It was what I\u2019ve heard some helicopter pilots call a brownout, and I stumbled forward, unable to see more than a half a metre or less in front of my face. There were shapes and shadows, twists of something here and there. I might even have heard voices, but I wasn\u2019t sure. I couldn\u2019t smell anything either, as my nose clogged up with grit. I tried breathing through my mouth as I pushed my way through the cloud, turning my head to the right as I was sure I heard something. Maybe even Gaba\u2019s voice, shouting for me to get down. \u201cOn the ground!\u201d Not Gaba. He wouldn\u2019t shout at me in English. \u201cOn the damn ground, lady!\u201d And he would never call me a lady. And then, when two bulky shadows charged through the cloud and knocked me off my feet, I knew it wasn\u2019t Gaba, and I guessed, from their voices and their actions, the Americans had arrived. It didn\u2019t matter that my nose was stuffed and clogged. I was never going to be able to breathe with the American kneeling on my chest. \u201cGet off her, now!\u201d I heard the words but considering the lack of oxygen getting to my brain, it could have been a hallucination. As it turned out, it was Gaba. There was a scuffle, and I heard more shouting, more cursing, and the promise of wicked things happening to one man and then the other as a brawl broke out in the cloud of dust around the Viking longhouse. I rolled onto my belly and crawled away from the men fighting around me, barely aware that I had lost both my weapons for the second time in as many hours. But as I crawled, choking on gritty air, the helicopters settled in a farmer\u2019s field to the left of the longhouse. The dust settled too, and I picked myself up, spitting grit and phlegm from my mouth, then wiping my lips with the back of my hand.

SCENE: VIKING-LONGHOUSE; MORNING; TENSE
RACHAEL: "YOU CAN’T GET IN MY WAY, CONSTABLE."
RACHAEL: "I’VE COME THIS FAR; WILL SEE TO END."
NARRATOR: "SO AM I."
ACTION: RACHAEL SWITCHES FIRE-SELECTOR(SAFE→SINGLE-SHOT)
CTX: GABA CALL="LIGHT WEIGHT" ∴ MP5 LIGHT-WEIGHT
CTX: ATII GIFT=LIGHT-MP5(EMPTY-MAG ∵ PREVENT-UNINTENDED-KILL)
INTENT: NARRATOR ¬WANT KILL RACHAEL
TRIGGER: RACHAEL STEALS PANIULA-DIARY ∧ NEED DIARY ∵ UILIUIT ALERT
TRAIT: NARRATOR=IMPULSIVE
ACTION: NARRATOR GRABS MP5-BARREL
RACHAEL: "HONEY, STOP!"
ACTION: NARRATOR YANKS BARREL ∧ TRUST EMPTY-MAG ∧ GABA-WHISPER LIGHT.WEIGHT
CONFLICT: RACHAEL(TUG) ∧ NARRATOR(TUG) → CLICK(TRIGGER)
RACHAEL: "OH, SHIT!"×3
ACTION: RACHAEL RELEASES GUN ∧ STUMBLES ∧ FALLS
SIDE-EFFECT: PISTOL FROM JEANS-POCKET SKITTERS
NARRATOR: "IT'S OKAY; YOU DIDN'T SHOOT; I'M NOT HURT."
RACHAEL PANIC MODE → RUNS FOR DOOR
OBSERVE: MORNING-LIGHT → PISTOL ON FLOOR
ACTION: NARRATOR RETRIEVES & HOLSTERS PISTOL
HEAR: HELICOPTER NOISE OUTSIDE LONGHOUSE
NARRATOR: "MOVE; SHE'S STILL GOT DIARY!"
OBSERVE: HELICOPTER×2 → BROWNOUT DUST-CLOUD ENGULFS
EFFECT: VISIBILITY≈0.5M ∧ HEARING MUFFLED-VOICES
ACTION: NARRATOR PUSHES THROUGH DUST
DANGER: TWO BULKY SHADOWS CHARGE → NARRATOR KNOCKED DOWN
AMERICAN VOICE: "ON THE DAMN GROUND, LADY!"
SCUFFLE: AMERICAN KNEELS CHEST → NARRATOR CHOKES
GABA: "GET OFF HER NOW!" → BRAWL ERUPTS
ACTION: NARRATOR CRAWLS AWAY ∧ LOSES WEAPONS AGAIN
OBSERVE: HELICOPTERS LAND FIELD-LEFT ∧ DUST SETTLES
ACTION: NARRATOR RISES ∧ SPITS GRIT ∧ WIPES LIPS
